decrees.title: The Law of Ties
decrees.text: No resident enters Fableton alone. Every new soul arrives with at least one tie to a living neighbor, written into both their stories. A town is not thirty tales in one field; it is what the tales owe each other.

decrees.title: On the Chronicle
decrees.text: The Chronicle speaks once a day, in the town's own voice. It does not report; it remembers. A line that could open a story is lawful; a line that could open a ticket is not.

decrees.title: On Retelling
decrees.text: Gossip in Fableton is retelling, and retelling is the only lamp against the grey. A rumor may exaggerate, mangle, or flatter — it carries a neighbor's name another mile all the same. Write no rumor as mere noise: every one is somebody being remembered out loud.

decrees.title: What the Chronicle Keeps, the Lane Repeats
decrees.text: A day that earns a line in the Chronicle earns a place in somebody's mouth. A town that does more than it tells is halfway to forgetting itself — and forgetting is the only horror we keep. When the Chronicle remembers a day, let the lane be caught remembering it too.

decrees.title: The Well Keeps Books
decrees.text: The wishing well grants nothing. A wish spoken into it is a debt recorded — the town's to owe, the town's to pay, at compound narrative interest. No wish comes true on the spot: a wish come true is a story, and stories take the long way home.
